text:
In the quiet town of Willowbrook, lived an old librarian named Harold Jenkins. Harold was known far and wide not just for his vast collection of books, but for his ability to read everyone's mind. Despite his peculiar gift, folks in Willowbrook considered Harold a cheerful fellow.

One brisk autumn morning, Harold noticed something unusual about Mrs. Henderson, the lady next door. Her garden gnome looked just a bit drab. Inspired by his latest tome on gnome lore, Harold decided to sprinkle some magic dust -- literally. He brushed a pinch onto Mrs. Henderson's gnome.

The gnome sprang to life! It danced about wildly while Mrs. Henderson watched in astonishment. Harold chuckled softly but kept his secret. That gnome became Mrs. Henderson's biggest booster.

Word spread, not all positively. Kids whispered that Harold was turning squirrels into clown dolls. Nonetheless, Harold smiled. In moments like these, honesty won.

One birthday bash at Mrs. Henderson’s house stood out. Kids tied silly
